text: The surgeon encounters filmy adhesions near the right hepatic margin, with the duodenum visibly creeping up slightly in the surgical field.
The cystic duct becomes visible in the background as the surgeon establishes the critical view of safety, with the posterior branch of the artery also coming into view.
The surgeon identifies the cystic duct and cystic artery, with visible fat fusing the gallbladder laterally to the right hepatic margin. The narrow cystic plate is also observed.
